data.title: ECB Chief Economist Lane: September Will Be the Next Key Policy Node
data.description:
ECB Chief Economist Philip R. Lane said the European Central Bank will reassess and possibly adjust its policy stance in September, with interest-rate decisions continuing to depend on economic data. According to Jin10, he said the current situation is a "medium-sized shock" and that whether policy is adjusted in September depends largely on whether oil and gas prices can stay high or whether a lasting solution can be found to reopen supply through the Strait of Hormuz.

Lane also said the European economy is mainly supported by domestic demand, adding that Europe trades with the whole world and that the United States is not a dominant factor in international trade. He said the ECB had previously kept rates unchanged to assess the impact of the Middle East conflict on eurozone inflation and economic growth.

data.title: PE Founder Pleads Guilty to $50 Million Investor Fraud
data.description:
A private equity firm founder and former Republican candidate for New Hampshire governor pleaded guilty to defrauding investors of more than $50 million, according to Bloomberg.

The case centers on allegations that the founder misled investors and took money from them in a fraud scheme.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Launches Opus 5 AI Model at Half the Price of Fable 5
data.description: According to Jin10, Anthropic announced the launch of its Opus 5 AI model, which it said comes close to the frontier intelligence level of Fable 5, but costs only half as much.

data.title: STOCKS | Qualcomm Shares Fall More Than 1.5% After Reportedly Notifying Customers of Double-Digit Price Increases
data.description: Qualcomm shares fell more than 1.5% after reports said it notified customers that prices will rise by a double-digit percentage. According to Jin10, the move came as消息称其通知客户价格将上涨两位数百分比.

data.title: Zilliqa Suspends Native Transactions After Ledger Signature Flaw Disclosure
data.description:
Zilliqa suspended native transactions after disclosing that roughly five affected Ledger app signatures from the same private key may allow attackers to reconstruct that key. According to NS3.AI, the flaw affects native, non-EVM transactions signed through Zilliqa Ledger app versions released between 2019 and 2026.

Zilliqa detected on-chain activity consistent with active exploitation on July 19 and did not quantify any losses. The network has not announced a reopening date or a final migration procedure.

data.title: Houthis Say Bab al-Mandeb Strait Is Not Blocked After Red Sea Shipping Threat
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing AFP, a Houthi spokesman said the group had not blocked traffic through the strategic Bab al-Mandeb Strait after announcing this week a maritime blockade on enemy states, including Saudi Arabia. The Iran-backed group said its position was limited to a sea blockade affecting the Saudi side only, in response to what it called Saudi Arabia's blockade of Houthi-controlled areas.

The statement came after the Houthis attacked a Saudi vessel in the nearby Red Sea. The group said on radio Monday that it would attack ships belonging to the enemy unless they complied with the blockade order. AFP said data it analyzed on Wednesday showed nine ships turned back after the Houthis announced the blockade, although some later resumed course.

The Houthis' attack has added to turbulence in global energy markets as clashes between Iran and the United States flare again. Brent crude briefly surged above USD 100 a barrel on Thursday. Separately, Kpler data cited by AFP showed that Saudi Arabia's seaborne crude exports through the Bab al-Mandeb Strait from March 2026 to mid-July 2026 were eight times higher than in the same period in 2025 after war broke out in the Middle East.

data.title: New Russian Oil Loading Halt Reported at Novorossiysk's Sheskharis Terminal
data.description: Oil loading at the Sheskharis oil terminal in Novorossiysk, Russia, has not taken place since Tuesday morning, July 21. According to Jin10, the terminal has remained without crude loading operations since then.

data.title: House Democrats Blast DCCC Over $514,000 Arizona Primary Spending
data.description: According to Axios, House Democrats are criticizing the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee after it spent more than $500,000 backing Marlene Galán-Woods in Arizona's 1st District, where former Arizona state Rep. Amish Shah was leading the Democratic primary by about seven percentage points with 87% of votes counted Wednesday evening. The DCCC's $514,000 joint ad buy with Galán-Woods followed its June endorsement of her in the party's Red to Blue program, and lawmakers including Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Rep. Delia Ramirez, Rep. Susie Lee, Rep. Melanie Stansbury and Rep. Ro Khanna said the party should focus its money on general elections instead of intervening in primaries. The dispute comes after similar DCCC-backed efforts failed in California's 22nd District and Maine's 2nd District, where the committee also spent roughly $250,000 in joint ads, according to AdImpact and Axios.

data.title: ECB's Lane Sets September Review on Rates
data.description: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said the bank will review, and potentially adjust, its policy stance in September, according to Bloomberg reported.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia’s Top Black Sea Oil Port Halts Loadings on Safety Fears
data.description:
Russia’s largest Black Sea oil port has paused tanker loadings over the past several days amid a surge in Ukrainian drone attacks in the area, according to Bloomberg.

The disruption affects a key export outlet as safety concerns rise around the port.

data.title: Iranian and Omani Foreign Ministers Discuss Strait of Hormuz Security Situation
data.description: A statement said Iran's and Oman's foreign ministers discussed the latest regional and security situation in the Strait of Hormuz, as well as maritime navigation issues, in a phone call. According to Jin10, the statement showed the two sides also discussed maritime navigation issues.

data.title: S&P 500 Rebounds as Earnings Offset War, Tech Bubble Fears
data.description: The S&P 500 Index rebounded Friday, ending a volatile week as generally strong corporate earnings helped steady markets rattled by the oil-price spike and concerns about an AI-driven tech stock rally, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Qualcomm Tells Customers Product Prices Will Rise by Double Digits
data.description: According to market sources, Qualcomm (QCOM.O) told customers that its product prices will be raised by a double-digit percentage. The company said it can no longer absorb rising costs because costs keep increasing.

data.title: Iranian Media: Iranian Military Chief Vows Retaliation for Each Iranian Killed
data.description: According to Iranian media, the head of Iran's Supreme Joint Military Command vowed that Iran will kill one U.S. military personnel for every Iranian killed.

data.title: Trump to Host Nuclear Energy CEOs for Reactor Milestone
data.description: US President Donald Trump is set to host leaders of advanced nuclear reactor companies at the White House on Friday, according to Bloomberg, as he touts their technology reaching a crucial milestone.

data.title: U.S. And U.K. Plan London Talks On Strait Of Hormuz Shipping Coalition
data.description:
According to Axios, the U.S. and U.K. are planning to convene a high-level meeting in London next week to discuss a potential international coalition to protect maritime shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The meeting, which is still being finalized, could bring together defense ministers and top military commanders from Western countries and regional states, and Defense Secretary Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Gen. Dan Caine could potentially attend. Axios said the effort is tied to reopening the strait for commercial shipping, which is seen as part of a U.S. exit strategy from the war with Iran and broader efforts to stabilize global energy markets.

Axios reported that Iran began attacking ships in the strait two weeks ago, prompting the U.S. to carry out daily strikes against Iranian military targets along Iran's southern coast. There have been no Iranian attacks on commercial ships in the last four days, though U.S. officials disagree on whether that reflects damage to Iran's capabilities or a still-existing ability to strike. President Trump also threatened on Wednesday to bomb bridges and power plants, including in Tehran, if Iran attacks more ships in the strait, while Iran threatened infrastructure in Gulf states allied with the U.S. Axios added that the escalation followed failed talks between Oman, Iran and Qatar on a new arrangement, and that an Omani delegation returned to Tehran on Friday to resume discussions on reopening the strait.

data.title: Crypto Groups Urge Senate to Back Clarity Act Draft
data.description: The Crypto Council for Innovation, Blockchain Association, and Digital Chamber urged Senate leaders to support the latest Clarity Act draft, citing about 67 million Americans who own digital assets. According to NS3.AI, the draft would ban officials and their families from issuing or promoting crypto. The House of Representatives has passed the bill, but it remains in deadlock over concerns tied to stablecoin yields.

data.title: Iranian Media Says Nearly All Commercial Vessels Transit the Bab el-Mandeb in Compliance with Yemeni Naval Decisions
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian media Fars News reported that a source linked to Yemen's maritime administration said almost all commercial ships passing through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait comply with the relevant decisions of Yemen's naval forces.

data.title: STOCKS | SLB Jumps Most in Over Six Months on 2027 Oil, Gas Investment Outlook
data.description:
SLB rose the most in more than six months after saying it expects constructive oil and gas investment in 2027, driven by supply disruptions from the conflict in the Middle East, according to Bloomberg.

The oil field services provider said the outlook is tied to the disruptions, which it sees supporting spending in the sector.

data.title: LeBron James Says He Is Joining the Philadelphia 76ers
data.description:
LeBron James said he is signing with the Philadelphia 76ers for his 24th NBA season, ending his run with the Los Angeles Lakers, according to Bloomberg.

James is the NBA’s all-time leading scorer.
